rulings.Case Title: KEN ELDER V. U.S. BANK, N.A.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Approval Of (1) Settlement Agreement And Dismissal Of Action; And (2) Relator'S Application For Relator'S Fee, And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Friday, February 7, 2025, line 11, PLAINTIFF KEN ELDER'S Motion For Approval Of (1) Settlement Agreement And Dismissal Of Action; And (2) Relator'S Application For Relator'S Fee Plaintiff and qui tam relator Ken Elder seeks an order approving a settlement agreement and dismissing this California False Claims Act (CFCA) action. The motion is unopposed and Elder's counsel provides the settlement agreement with defendant U.S. Bank N.A. and declares under penalty of perjury that the California Attorney General's Office consents to the settlement agreement and dismissal. The motion attaches as Exhibit C to the Golub declaration a written consent to dismissal signed by that office. For good cause shown in the moving papers, "taking into account the best interests of the parties involved and the public purposes behind" the CFCA (Cal. Gov. Code 12652(c)(1)), the court grants the motion. The court further finds the relator's fee of $6340.60 to be reasonable pursuant to Government Code 12652(g)(3). The action is dismissed. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CV)

rulings.Case Title: MATTHEW ROGIAN VS. UNAGI INC., A CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Friday, February 7, 2025, line 7, 1 - PLAINTIFF MATTHEW ROGIAN'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to Special Interrogatories Set One is GRANTED. The interrogatories at issue are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence in this action alleging, inter alia, product liability. (CCP Section 2017.010.) Plaintiff has shown that Defendant Unagi Inc.'s responses are incomplete. Specifically, in its responses, Unagi contended it had insufficient information to respond. In its opposing separate statement, Unagi has set forth additional unverified factual information about the scope of its search for information and what knowledge is in its possession, and has affirmatively stated that it does not have any responsive information. Plaintiff is entitled to receive that such an answer in verified responses. Unagi is required to provide information within its control, including information known to its agents and employees, such as court proceedings or complaints of which it is aware. Its initial responses did not unambiguously state that it was providing that responsive information. Plaintiff's meet and confer efforts were sufficient. Unagi shall provide further responses to Special Interrogatory Nos. 3 through 9 within 15 days of notice of entry of this order. The court does not find Unagi acted with substantial justification; accordingly, sanctions are mandatory. Defendant shall pay $860 to Plaintiff in sanctions, payment no later than 15 days from notice of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CV)

rulings.Case Title: MATTHEW ROGIAN VS. UNAGI INC., A CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Further Responses To Demands For Production And Notice Of Hearing
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Friday, February 7, 2025, line 7, 2 - PLAINTIFF MATTHEW ROGIAN'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Demands For Production And Notice Of Hearing The court grants in part and denies in part the motion to compel further responses to requests for production of documents. The court denies the request for further production as to request numbers 10, 11, 12, 13, and 16. Unagi's responses are in substantial compliance with its code obligations; Unagi has represented that it has no documents in its possession and Plaintiff offers no facts defeating that assertion. (That a lawsuit was filed in another jurisdiction is not to the contrary; Unagi does not necessarily have documents concerning that case and is not obliged to obtain documents from third parties who are not its agents to respond to the RFP.) The court denies the request as to RFP 15 (asking for battery incidents): Unagi's separate statement and opposing papers state that additional documents have been produced and that no further documents are responsive. The court grants the request as to the following RFPs: Unagi shall amend its response to RFP 14, asking for information about front wheel incidents, to state in its response that a reasonably diligent search was performed and no responsive documents were located, the straightforward representation it makes in its opposing separate statement (but not in its RFP response). The court notes that Plaintiff's apparent position that Unagi must produce records in the possession of nonparties who are not its agents or employees is incorrect. Plaintiff's meet and confer efforts were sufficient. Unagi shall provide further responses to the RFP as specified above within 15 days of notice of entry of this order. The court does not find that either party acted with substantial justification and does not award sanctions. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A. VS. BETHANY SHERBO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order That Mattters In Equest Fo Admission Of Truth Of Facts Be Deemed Admitted; Memorandum In Support Of Motion; Declaration In Support Of Motion
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Friday, February 7, 2025, line 10, PLAINTIFF JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A.'S Motion For Order That Mattters In Equest Fo Admission Of Truth Of Facts Be Deemed Admitted JP Morgan Chase N.A.'s unopposed motion to deem requests for admissions admitted is granted. The truth of any matters specified in the requests is deemed admitted in light of responding party's failure to respond to the requests for admissions before the date of hearing. See CCP 2033.280(b). CCP 2033.280(c) states that a court order for sanctions is mandatory in this circumstance. The court orders defendant Bethany Sherbo to pay $100 to JP Morgan Chase, by payment to its counsel, within 20 court days of the date of notice of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: J. DENNIS BONNEY ET AL VS. 1810 JACKSON STREET HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Friday, February 7, 2025, line 5, DEFENDANT 1810 JACKSON STREET HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT A motion to strike lies only where the pleading has irrelevant, false or improper matter, or has not been drawn or filed in conformity with laws. (Civ. Proc. Code 436.) A Motion to Strike is the proper procedure for testing the adequacy of a punitive damages allegation because punitive damages are not a cause of action. (Grieves v. Superior Ct. (1984) 157 Cal.App.3d 159, 164.) Punitive Damages are recoverable where the defendant has been guilty of oppression, fraud, or malice, express or implied. (Civ. Code 3294.) The Complaint alleges facts sufficient to state a claim for punitive damages. (See Cmplnt., 7:23-25.) Taking the allegations of the complaint as true, Plaintiff alleges that the Association Board moved up a scheduled vote on the apportionment of common area storage, and did not give notice of the scheduled vote, after being notified of plaintiff's application of ex parte relief to prevent the reapportionment of storage space. This allegation is a sufficient factual pleading demonstrating implied oppression, fraud, or malice. The motion to strike Prayer (page 12, line 6): "Judgment for exemplary damages in the sum to be proven at trial," is denied. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts.
